threads.title: Unfair Display Replacement Policy for Non-S Series Users

messages.author: febiiin
messages.subject: Unfair Display Replacement Policy for Non-S Series Users
messages.text:
I am really frustrated after hearing that Samsung will give free replacement only for S series phones, not for M, A, and F series.
 If it is a manufacturing defect, it can occur within two years. In my case, it occurred after two years and just a few hours after the January software update.
 This is to all Samsung users other than the S series;
Samsung treats us unfairly. They attract budget buyers with these series, but for a display change, they charge ₹8,000, and that too is not the user's fault.
 I am writing the above lines because, of course, I also got green lines on my display yesterday. I am very disappointed and not able to use the phone properly with these lines.
 The last Update I have done

messages.author: xSteveuk
messages.subject: Re: Latest June-July 2025 Updated UI 7.0 bricked my Samsung galaxy F54 with Gr..
messages.text:
Hi,
Troubleshooting Steps:
1. Check for Loose Connections:
In some cases, a loose display connector might be the culprit. You can try connecting the phone to a PC or other device and see if the lines disappear when pressing on the screen. 
2. Restart in Safe Mode:
Booting the phone in safe mode can help determine if a third-party app is causing the issue. If the lines disappear in safe mode, a conflicting app might be the problem. 
3. Wipe Cache Partition:
Clearing the cache partition can sometimes resolve software-related display glitches.

threads.title: Green Line Issue on Samsung F54 – Request for Official Solution

messages.author: its_your_oliver
messages.subject: Green Line Issue on Samsung F54 – Request for Official Solution
messages.text:
I am using a Samsung F54, and recently I have been facing a serious issue. A green vertical line suddenly appears on the display. It disappears sometimes but keeps coming back. My phone has not been dropped or mishandled, and this issue happened unexpectedly. 
 I visited local shops, and they suggested a screen replacement costing around ₹7k–8k, but without any guarantee that the problem will not return. 
 I have noticed that many Samsung users with different models are also facing similar issues, which seems like a possible manufacturing defect. I kindly request Samsung to look into this matter and provide an official solution or a free screen replacement policy for affected users. 
 I really trust the Samsung brand, and I hope the company will take responsibility and support its customers in this situation. @samsungindia

Samsung offers free screen replacements for some Galaxy phones under specific circumstances, primarily related to a known green line display issue.
Conditions
(e.g., 3 years from purchase) and without physical or water damage.
Limited to certain models and regions, including the Galaxy S21 series, S21 FE, S22 Ultra, and extended to S23 as well due to the growing number of users facing the green line issue on their devices.
The offer is valid until 30th September 2025
If you find this helpful, I'd appreciate it if you could ACCEPT it as a SOLUTION. Thank you 😊

messages.author: the_aghori
messages.subject: Green line updates for F54
messages.text:
My Samsung phone has developed green lines on the display after a software update. On inspection, I found that only one LDI strip near the USB port shows a faint pink tint, while all other LDIs are perfectly white. The phone has never been exposed to water, rain, or liquid spills. There are no corrosion marks on the USB port or inside the device. Samsung LDIs are known to be overly sensitive and may react to heat from charging or normal humidity, which could explain this faint discoloration. Therefore, this cannot be considered genuine water damage, and my case should not be rejected on that basis. 
 https://xdaforums.com/t/tip-keep-your-ldi-from-voiding-your-warranty-due-to-false-water-indications.1198017/?utm_source=perplexity 
 https://www.reddit.com/r/samsung/comments/vxp5dd/can_we_please_discuss_samsungs_massively_faulty/?utm_source=perplexity 
 My single advice to everyone is please get rid of the company the doesnot value customers...

Samsung Customer Support

messages.author: cs_member8
messages.subject: Re: Green line issue in F54 5G
messages.text:
Dear Samsung Member,
 Greetings from Samsung Customer Support!
 At Samsung, we always aim to provide impeccable services to our customers, and we highly regret that you feel this way.
 Please follow these easy troubleshooting steps which may help in resolving this issue:
 ● Kindly restart your device.
 ● Update software on device: apps screen > Settings > Software update > Download and install > Download > Install now.
 ● Reset the device:
Note: Kindly create data backup of your device via SMART switch mobile application or http://www.samsung.com/in/support/smartswitch pc suite. Remember Google password before resetting the device because it required during initial setup. If you don't remember the password, then reset (Forgot password) your google account.
 Settings > General Management > Reset > Temporary cloud backup or External storage backup > Factory data reset (This will erase all data, including files and downloaded apps.)
 If the issue still occurs, please visit the service centre for a physical inspection of your device. You can locate your nearest service center with this given link: https://www.samsung.com/in/support/service-center/ 
 Warm Regards,
Samsung Customer Support

threads.title: Green Line Appeared After Update on Galaxy F54 5G — No Support from Samsung

messages.author: Maulik01
messages.subject: Green Line Appeared After Update on Galaxy F54 5G — No Support from Samsung
messages.text:
Hello Samsung Team and Community, I’m using Galaxy F54 5G and have always trusted Samsung. My warranty expired just on 9th June 2025, and on 22nd July, after a software update, a green line suddenly appeared on my screen. Now it's increasing daily, even though my phone has no physical or water damage. 
 I raised a complaint via Samsung India Instagram, shared my details, and followed up — but I haven’t received any real support yet. 
 I belong to a middle-class family, and we trusted Samsung so much that we have 3 Samsung phones and even a Samsung washing machine at home. But still, I’m not getting the kind of support that S-series users get for this same issue. Why are only premium users eligible for free display replacement, when this is clearly a software or display defect? 
 I kindly request Samsung to offer a goodwill-based free screen replacement, as this issue is not user-caused. Please help. 
 Thanks, Maulik Parekh Ahmedabad, Gujarat 
 (view in My Videos)

threads.title: Green line issue on samsung f54 5g

messages.author: jaikrishn_j
messages.subject: Green line issue on samsung f54 5g
messages.text:
I purchased my first samsung phone(f54) a year ago and it was working fine till i updated my phone recently to one ui 6.1 . From then onwards it is a nightmare. I started seeing green line multiple times in different places. Sometimes it just vanishes and come back again in a different spot. I am pretty sure that i haven't dropped my phone or water damaged it. I am more like a laptop guy so i use my phone very less comparatively. Most of the time my phone is at my home and i barely use it outside my home. I contacted samsung authorized service center and they can only replace the screen at it will cost me  7500INR which is basically 25% of what my phone cost (approx). I searched on many forums and found that samsung is offering free replacement to the S series smartphones for the same issue and yet they are not providing any for my phone series. I researched a little more and found out that the green line issue is  due to hardware damage and it might be caused due to overheating while the phone is updating(might be the reason for me). I digged a little more deeper and found that the S series phones uses the same screen technology (AMOLED) of what my phone uses. After finding out, i dont see why Samsung refuses to offer free screen replacement for other phone series which uses the same screen technology and the same OS . Even after multiple request and talking with service center they are unwilling to help me with this issue.  
 I personally used to like samsung as a brand and for there products. Everyone in my family uses a samsung phone. But after what i am currently facing with there products i strongly regret even considering samsung when i was planning to buy a new phone. I personally thinks that these phone manufacturers like samsung wants us to keep changing our phones after year or so and for that they refuses to offer privileges like these to other phone series segments which comes under the S series.

messages.author: cs_member10
messages.subject: Re: Green line in Galaxy F54
messages.text:
Dear Samsung Member,
 Greetings from Samsung Customer Support!
 We want to inform you that it is our best endeavor to provide you with an impeccable product experience at Samsung. Our products pass through rigorous quality checks which are globally recognized. The issue could occur due to usage and various factors, in case there is any kind of issue that occurs after the update then we are always helping our customers to resolve their issues. If anyone facing a line issue on their devices. So, kindly perform the following steps to resolve your issue.
 Kindly restart your device once: To power off through Quick settings, swipe down from the top of the screen. Tap the Power icon in the upper right of the screen (next to the Settings icon), and then tap Power off.
 You can reset your device by following the steps.
1. Kindly create a data backup of your device via the SMART switch mobile application or http://www.samsung.com/in/support/smartswitch pc suite.
2. Remember the Google password before resetting the device because it is required during initial setup.
3. If you do not remember then advise to reset (Forgot password) his/her Google account.
 Path: Settings> General Management.>Reset>Temporary cloud backup or External storage backup>After backup tab on Factory data reset to reset your device to its factory default settings.
This will erase all data, including files and downloaded apps.
 If the issue still occurs, please visit the service center for a physical inspection of your device. You can locate your nearest service center with this given link: https://www.samsung.com/in/support/service-center/ 
 Warm Regards,
Samsung Customer Support

messages.author: AvishekG
messages.subject: Re: Green Line Issue Galaxy F54 5G
messages.text:
🔧 Troubleshooting Steps
1. Restart Your Device: A simple restart can sometimes resolve minor software glitches.
2. Boot into Safe Mode:
Press and hold the Power button.
Tap and hold "Power off" until "Safe Mode" appears.
Tap "Safe Mode" to restart.
If the green line disappears, a third-party app may be causing the issue.
3. Clear Cache Partition:
Turn off your device.
Press and hold the Volume Up and Power buttons simultaneously.
When the Samsung logo appears, release the buttons.
Use the volume buttons to navigate to "Wipe cache partition" and confirm with the Power button.
After completion, select "Reboot system now".
4. Test Display Using Samsung's Diagnostic Code:
Open the Phone app.
Dial *#0*# to access the diagnostic menu.
Test the Red, Green, and Blue screens.
If the green line appears during these tests, it's likely a hardware issue.
5. Factory Reset (Last Resort):
Back up your data.
Go to Settings > General Management > Reset > Factory Data Reset.
If the issue persists after a reset, it's likely hardware-related.
 🛠 ️ Hardware Consideration
The green line issue is often due to a hardware fault, such as a damaged display connector or internal component failure. Overheating during updates can exacerbate this problem.
If you find this helpful, kindly accept it as a Solution. It helps others
Tap the 3 dots on my reply and select "Accept Solution." Thank you 😊

Samsung offers free screen replacements for some Galaxy phones under specific circumstances, primarily related to a known green line display issue.
Conditions
(e.g., 3 years from purchase) and without physical or water damage.
Limited to certain models and regions, including the Galaxy S21 series, S21 FE, S22 Ultra, and extended to S23 as well due to the growing number of users facing the green line issue on their devices.
The offer is valid until 31st December 2025

messages.author: cs_member10
messages.subject: Re: One red and one green line on samsung  galaxy f54 5g  display after updating  the UI Version -6.
messages.text:
Dear Samsung Member,
 Greetings from Samsung Customer Support!
 As per your query, we would request you to kindly perform the following steps to resolve your issue.
 Kindly restart your device once: To power off through Quick settings, swipe down from the top of the screen. Tap the Power icon in the upper right of the screen (next to the Settings icon), and then tap Power off.
 Update the software if available: https://www.samsung.com/in/support/mobile-devices/samsung-galaxy-smartphone-how-to-update-software/ 
 Reset your device: https://www.samsung.com/in/support/mobile-devices/samsung-galaxy-mobile-how-to-perform-a-factory-reset/ 
 If the issue still occurs, please visit the service centre for a physical inspection of your device. You can locate your nearest service centre with this given link: https://www.samsung.com/in/support/service-center/ 
 Warm Regards,
Samsung Customer Support

messages.author: cs_member10
messages.subject: Re: Greenline issue
messages.text:
Dear Samsung Member,
 Greetings from Samsung Customer Support!
 At Samsung, we always aim to provide impeccable services to our customers, and we highly regret that you feel this way.
 Please follow these easy troubleshooting steps which may help in resolving this issue:
 ● Kindly restart your device.
 ● Update software on device: apps screen > Settings > Software update > Download and install > Download > Install now.
 ● Reset the device:
Note: Kindly create data backup of your device via SMART switch mobile application or http://www.samsung.com/in/support/smartswitch pc suite. Remember Google password before resetting the device because it required during initial setup. If you don't remember the password, then reset (Forgot password) your google account.
 Settings > General Management > Reset > Temporary cloud backup or External storage backup > Factory data reset (This will erase all data, including files and downloaded apps.)
 If the issue still occurs, please visit the service centre for a physical inspection of your device. You can locate your nearest service centre with this given link: https://www.samsung.com/in/support/service-center/ 
 Warm Regards,
Samsung Customer Support

messages.author: Kan09
messages.subject: Display Issue in F54
messages.text: I think there is a display issue while using the phone in low brightness. Some sort of white pixels come up while dragging black background like youtube settings. Anyone facing this?

messages.author: cs_member10
messages.subject: Re: Display Issue in F54
messages.text:
Dear Samsung member,
 Greetings from Samsung customer support.
 We acknowledge your query and apologize for the inconvenience caused to you and will surely assist you regarding the same. Please follow the easy troubleshooting steps which might help to solve the issue.
 Check the device in safe mode: Power off the device>Press and hold the Power key for one or two seconds to turn on the device>>When the Samsung logo appears, press and hold the Volume down key.
 Note: If your device functions correctly in Safe Mode, it is most likely that the issue lies with a 3rd party application that can be uninstalled to resolve the problem.
 To exit the safe mode, restart the device.
 Update device software: Settings >> Software update >> Download and install>>Check for software update >> If Software update available>>Download>>Downloading updates>>Install now.
Note: Your phone will restart after the update is installed.
 Reset the device:
Note: Kindly create a data backup of your device via smart switch mobile application or http://www.samsung.com/in/support/smartswitch pc suite.
Remember the google password before resetting the device because it is required during the initial setup.
 If you do not remember then advise to reset (Forgot password) the google account.
For this; Open Gmail ( https://accounts.google.com/servicelogin )>>Select forgot password>>Select "try another way" like mobile number, security question etc.>>Select Next>>Enter a new password>>Tap on Next.
 Apps>settings> general management>reset (Reset your device to its factory default settings. This will erase all data, including files and downloaded apps.)
 In case the issue still occurs. We request you to kindly visit the service center for a physical inspection of your device. You can locate your nearest service center with this given link: https://www.samsung.com/in/support/service-center/ 
 Thank you for writing to us.
Warm Regards,
Samsung Customer Support.
